project: User ID Prefixed Skill Artifact Isolation
description: Enforce strict per-user isolation for thread views, thread interactions, memory JSON, uploads, artifacts, custom skills, skill state, MCP config, and all Skill Artifact storage using authenticated user ID prefixes with no global fallback.

userStories.title: Add authenticated user context dependency
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want a reusable dependency that returns the verified current user ID for resource routes so that every isolated resource uses server-authenticated identity.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a helper or dependency that decodes the session cookie, verifies token_version, and returns the current user ID as a string
The helper raises 401 when the cookie is missing, invalid, expired, or points to a missing user
The helper does not read user_id from request body, query params, metadata, config, configurable, or headers
Existing get_optional_user_id behavior remains available only for explicitly optional or legacy paths
Add tests covering valid cookie, missing cookie, invalid token, and token_version mismatch
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add canonical user resource prefix helpers
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want canonical user prefix helpers so that Store keys and filesystem resource identifiers use one validated user namespace format.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a canonical user_prefix(user_id) helper that returns '{userID}: ' after validating the user ID
Add helper functions for prefixed thread keys, memory keys, extension config keys, and artifact path labels
User ID validation rejects empty IDs, path traversal, slashes, backslashes, and unsafe characters
Filesystem-safe mapping is documented and covered by tests if physical paths cannot directly use colon and trailing space
Add unit tests for valid IDs, invalid IDs, logical prefix output, and stable filesystem-safe mapping if used
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create strict thread ownership service
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want a single thread ownership service so that all thread routes consistently create and verify ownership before reading data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add service functions to bind a thread to an authenticated user using a user-prefixed Store key
Add service function require_thread_owner(request, thread_id) that returns the authenticated user ID or raises 401/404
Owner check happens before reading checkpoint values, thread metadata, uploads, artifacts, or run records
Unowned threads are rejected in strict multi-user mode
Client-provided owner metadata is ignored or overwritten by server-authenticated user ID
Add tests for owner match, owner mismatch, missing owner mapping, and malicious client user_id metadata
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce owner checks on thread CRUD and state routes
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want thread detail, search, state, history, patch, and delete routes to only operate on my own threads so that direct thread_id access cannot cross users.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/threads creates or binds the thread using the authenticated user ID from the server
POST /api/threads/search returns only threads under the current user prefix
GET /api/threads/{thread_id} checks ownership before returning metadata or values
GET and POST /api/threads/{thread_id}/state check ownership before reading or writing checkpoints
POST /api/threads/{thread_id}/history checks ownership before listing history
PATCH and DELETE /api/threads/{thread_id} check ownership before modifying or deleting
Cross-user access returns 404 or the chosen uniform denial status without leaking title or metadata
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce owner checks on runs routes
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want run creation, streaming, waiting, joining, listing, reading, and cancelling to be limited to my own threads so that active agent execution cannot cross users.
acceptanceCriteria: All /api/threads/{thread_id}/runs endpoints call the thread ownership service before creating, reading, joining, streaming, or cancelling runs
Stateless /api/runs endpoints bind generated thread IDs to the current user before running
Stateless /api/runs endpoints reject caller-supplied thread_id values owned by another user
start_run injects metadata.user_id from the authenticated server user and overwrites any client-provided user_id
RunManager list/get/cancel responses are filtered or rejected by thread owner
Add tests for cross-user run create, wait, join, cancel, and client-supplied metadata.user_id override
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Route main LangGraph traffic through authenticated Gateway path
userStories.description: As a system operator, I want the main chat path to pass through authenticated Gateway owner checks so that frontend chat, state, and runs cannot bypass isolation by talking to unauthenticated LangGraph Server routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Document and implement the selected routing strategy for /api/langgraph traffic: Gateway-compatible endpoints or equivalent LangGraph auth
Default local and Docker configuration no longer exposes unauthenticated state/runs/history paths for browser traffic
Frontend LangGraph SDK base URL uses the authenticated route by default
Existing streaming behavior still works through the authenticated route
Add a regression test or config assertion showing the default route does not bypass Gateway owner checks
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Isolate frontend thread view and caches by user
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want frontend thread lists, current thread view, stream reconnection, and cached state to reset or scope by user so that switching accounts never shows another user's data.
acceptanceCriteria: Thread list query keys include current user ID or are fully cleared on logout and user change
Current chat route verifies thread accessibility and redirects or shows an inaccessible state when the thread is not owned by the user
useStream reconnectOnMount/sessionStorage keys cannot reuse a run ID across different users
Logout clears thread, state, history, artifact, upload, and run-related caches
Two users in separate browser sessions do not see each other's thread list or direct thread page content
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make memory API strict per-user with no global fallback
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want memory endpoints to read and write only my user-prefixed memory JSON so that no other user's memory is visible or mutable.
acceptanceCriteria: All memory endpoints require authenticated user ID before reading or writing memory
Memory storage path or key uses the canonical user prefix for the authenticated user
GET /api/memory/status returns the authenticated user's memory data, not global memory
Memory import/export only targets the authenticated user's prefix
Missing authenticated user returns 401 and does not read global memory
Tests cover user A and user B writing different memory facts without cross-read
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make memory middleware and prompt injection strict per-user
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want automatic memory updates and prompt memory injection to use only the run owner's user-prefixed memory so that agent context never leaks across users.
acceptanceCriteria: MemoryMiddleware extracts user_id only from trusted server-injected runtime context or metadata
MemoryMiddleware does not write to global memory when user_id is missing in strict mode
Lead agent prompt memory injection loads memory with the authenticated run owner user_id
Agent creation fails or disables memory injection when no trusted user_id is available in strict mode
Tests prove user A memory is not injected into user B prompt
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add user-prefixed thread filesystem paths
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want thread-local workspace, uploads, outputs, and acp-workspace host paths to include the authenticated user's prefix so that artifact files cannot collide across users.
acceptanceCriteria: Paths exposes user-prefixed helpers for thread workspace, uploads, outputs, user-data root, and acp-workspace
ensure_thread_dirs creates user-prefixed directories for authenticated runs
resolve_virtual_path resolves /mnt/user-data paths under the authenticated user's prefix
delete_thread_dir deletes only the authenticated user's prefixed thread directory
The same thread_id under two different users maps to different host paths
Tests cover path creation, virtual path resolution, deletion, and same thread_id under two users
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update sandbox and thread-data middleware for user-prefixed paths
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want sandbox mounts and thread data middleware to mount only my user-prefixed thread directories so that agent execution cannot read another user's workspace or outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: ThreadDataMiddleware uses trusted user_id when creating and reporting workspace/uploads/outputs paths
Sandbox provider acquire/get/reconcile keys include user prefix or otherwise distinguish users with the same thread_id
Sandbox mounts for workspace, uploads, outputs, and acp-workspace use user-prefixed host paths
When trusted user_id is missing in strict mode, sandbox/thread-data setup fails instead of using global thread paths
Tests cover same thread_id under two users producing distinct sandbox mount sources
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce upload and artifact owner checks with user-prefixed paths
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want upload and artifact endpoints to check thread ownership and resolve paths under my user prefix so that knowing another user's artifact URL is not enough to access files.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/threads/{thread_id}/uploads checks owner before writing files
GET /api/threads/{thread_id}/uploads/list checks owner before listing files
DELETE /api/threads/{thread_id}/uploads/{filename} checks owner before deleting files
GET /api/threads/{thread_id}/artifacts/{path} checks owner before resolving normal paths
GET /api/threads/{thread_id}/artifacts/{path} checks owner before extracting files from .skill archives
Upload responses and artifact resolution use user-prefixed physical paths
Tests cover cross-user upload list, delete, artifact download, and .skill archive read denial
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make custom skill CRUD user-prefixed
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want installed and edited custom skills plus their history to live under my user prefix so that my skill changes never affect other users.
acceptanceCriteria: Custom skill list/get/update/delete endpoints require authenticated user ID
Custom skill install writes to the authenticated user's prefixed custom skills directory
Custom skill history and rollback read and write under the authenticated user's prefix
Global custom skill manager helpers are not used for authenticated custom skill CRUD unless they accept user_id and resolve user-prefixed paths
Two users can install or edit custom skills with the same name without overwriting each other
Tests cover same skill name under two users, independent history, and cross-user read denial
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make skill state and prompt injection user-prefixed
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want skill enablement and available skill prompt injection to use my user-prefixed configuration so that my Skill set is private and independent.
acceptanceCriteria: Skill enable/disable endpoint writes to the authenticated user's prefixed extensions config
load_skills(enabled_only=True, user_id=current_user_id) is used for authenticated prompt injection
Enabled skills cache is keyed by user_id or removed in favor of user-scoped loading
Agent prompt available_skills only includes skills visible and enabled for the run owner
User A disabling a public skill does not disable it for user B
Tests cover per-user skill enablement and prompt skill list isolation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make agents and user profile strict per-user
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want custom agents, SOUL.md, and USER.md to be read and written only under my user prefix so that agent configuration does not leak across users.
acceptanceCriteria: All /api/agents endpoints require authenticated user ID and use user-prefixed agent paths
All /api/user-profile endpoints require authenticated user ID and use the user-prefixed USER.md path
list_custom_agents(user_id) passes user_id through to load_agent_config for each scanned agent
Lead agent factory loads agent config and SOUL.md using the trusted run owner user_id
Authenticated agent/profile routes do not fall back to global agents or global USER.md
Tests cover user A and user B creating same agent name with different SOUL.md content
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make MCP config user-prefixed with optional read-only global template
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want MCP configuration and secrets to be isolated under my user prefix unless an administrator explicitly provides a read-only global template.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/mcp/config requires authenticated user ID for user-owned MCP config
PUT /api/mcp/config or replacement endpoints write only to the authenticated user's prefixed MCP config
Global MCP templates, if supported, are read-only for ordinary users
Ordinary users cannot read or write another user's MCP env, headers, OAuth tokens, client secret, or refresh token
Agent MCP tool loading uses the run owner's user-prefixed MCP config merged with allowed read-only global templates
Tests cover per-user MCP enabled state and secret isolation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove authenticated global fallback paths
userStories.description: As a system maintainer, I want authenticated resource access to fail instead of silently falling back to global paths so that old global data cannot leak into new user sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated memory, agent, profile, skill, upload, artifact, and MCP paths fail when trusted user_id is unavailable
Existing user_id=None global fallback remains only behind an explicit legacy or single-user mode setting if kept at all
Default configuration disables legacy global fallback for authenticated resource routes
Code comments or docs identify any remaining intentionally public/global resources
Tests assert authenticated resource access does not read .deer-flow/memory.json, .deer-flow/agents, global skills/custom, or global extensions_config.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add migration or cleanup command for legacy global data
userStories.description: As a system operator, I want an explicit migration or cleanup path for legacy global data so that strict isolation can be enabled without accidentally exposing old shared resources.
acceptanceCriteria: Add documentation or a script describing how to clear legacy global memory, agents, custom skills, extensions config, thread directories, and checkpoints
If migration is implemented, it requires an explicit target user ID and never guesses ownership
Migration or cleanup outputs a summary of changed, skipped, and failed resources
The default application startup does not auto-migrate or auto-read legacy global data
Tests or dry-run output demonstrate no implicit legacy data adoption
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add cross-user isolation regression tests
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated cross-user tests covering thread, memory, artifact, skill, and MCP boundaries so that regressions are caught before deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests create two authenticated users and assert thread list/detail/state/history isolation
Tests assert cross-user runs, uploads, artifact downloads, and .skill archive reads are denied
Tests assert memory writes and prompt injection are isolated between users
Tests assert custom skill content/history and skill enablement are isolated between users
Tests assert MCP config and secrets are isolated between users
Tests assert authenticated routes do not use global fallback resources
Typecheck passes
